raw.objective: “to evaluate the influence of patient-related factors and comedications on LTG CL/F”
raw.covariates: Tested: - Included: dose, SEX, AGE, WT, comedication with inducers (PHB, PHT, CBZ) or VPA
raw.observations: Many covariates were identified, including age. Using a linear model here may result in problems in terms of for example auto-induction based on dose, as it may not be extrapolatable outside the dose-range seen in their data. No population-PK approach was used.
raw.equations: intercept=theta1 Fdose=theta2 Find=theta3 FVPA=theta4 if(VPA==0 & IND==0) CO=0 if(VPA==0 & IND==1) CO=Find if(VPA==1 & IND==0) CO=FVPA CL=intercept+Fdose*DD+CO Css=DD/CL VPA is comedication with valproic acid (0=no; 1=yes) IND is comedication with PHB, PHT, or CBZ (0=no; 1=yes) DD is daily dose (mg/day)

extraction_target.title: An investigation of the influence of patient-related factors and comedications on lamotrigine clearance in patients with epilepsy.
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to evaluate the influence of patient-related factors and comedications on LTG CL/F”

covariates.raw: Tested: - Included: dose, SEX, AGE, WT, comedication with inducers (PHB, PHT, CBZ) or VPA

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
